g.text: Theophrastus's recipe for lead white is incomplete

g.title: katsaros_liritzis_laskaris_2010.T202024
g.text: Theophrastus's recipe for lead white lacks an
exact description of the jar and its cover

g.title: katsaros_liritzis_laskaris_2010.T201040
g.text: We combined Theophrastus's recipe for lead white

g.title: katsaros_liritzis_laskaris_2010.T124253
g.text: We reproduced in laboratory the white powder
sample PSI-1 (which is composed of cerussite and
hydrocerussite, and is therefore lead white)
preparation of lead white): .We cut commercial
metallic lead in the shape of a brick {approx. 10
cm length by 5 cm width by 2 cm thickness}, .We
placed vinegar on the bottom of a ceramic vessel,
.We hung the brick-shaped piece of lead over the
vinegar within the ceramic vessel, .We covered the
vessel with a skin {approx. 1 mm (in thickness)}
quasi-transparent to air (i.e., such that it
breathes), .We placed the vessel under the sun,
average 30 °C and a range of 27-55 degrees}, .We
removed the skin cover, .We took the brick-shaped
lead out, .We removed the white powdered layer
(thus formed) on the surface of the lead by
scratching, .In a marble mortar, we washed and
ground down to a fine powder the white powdered
layer using a marble pestle, .We waited for it to
dry out under the sun, ( .We obtained the white
powder sample PSI-1 (which is composed of
cerussite and hydrocerussite, and is therefore
the preparation of lead white) ), .We applied it
on a prepared wooden surface with a gypsum OR

g.title: katsaros_liritzis_laskaris_2010.T203426

complete method for the preparation of lead white
